title: Interpret Off-End Sort Confirmations To Track Parcel Destination
summary: Use parcel sort confirmation information to determine which reported off-end destination an off-end parcel went to. The source states that parcels going off the end of the sorter can still be tracked through sort confirmations indicating one of two off-end destinations, until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
when_to_use: Use when a parcel did not sort to a chute and tote and instead went off the end of the sorter, and you need to determine the reported off-end destination from available sort confirmation information.
not_for: Do not use to invent or assume the exact two off-end destination names when they are not provided in the source.
Do not use to define downstream handling beyond what the source states.
Do not use when no sort confirmation is available for the parcel.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review parcel sort confirmation information and interpret whether an off-end destination is reported.

safety_notes: This is a reference interpretation task based on system information; no physical intervention is supported by the source.
Do not infer unsupported parcel handling actions or destination labels from incomplete source evidence.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to sort confirmation information for the parcel

access_required: Access to parcel sort confirmation information
system_preconditions: A parcel of interest has gone off the end of the sorter rather than sorting to a chute and tote.
Sort confirmation information is available for the parcel.

steps.title: Identify the off-end parcel
steps.purpose: Confirm that the parcel being investigated is an off-end parcel rather than a normal chute-and-tote sort.
steps.instruction: Identify the parcel that did not sort to a chute and tote and instead went off the end of the sorter.
steps.expected_result: The parcel under review is confirmed as an off-end parcel.
steps.healthy_condition: The parcel is known to have gone off the end of the sorter and is the correct parcel for review.
steps.failure_condition: It is unclear whether the parcel actually went off the end, or the parcel under review cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Confirm off-end parcel case
actions.exact_action: Identify the parcel that did not sort to a chute and tote and instead went off the end of the sorter.

stop_or_escalate_if: The parcel cannot be confidently identified.
There is no basis to conclude the parcel went off the end of the sorter.

source_evidence.text: Identify the parcel that did not sort to a chute and tote and instead went off the end of the sorter.
Off the end goes into a Gaylord at Hazleton; sort confirmations indicate two destinations off the end, allowing some tracking until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.

steps.title: Check sort confirmation for off-end reporting
steps.purpose: Determine whether the parcel has sort confirmation data that reports an off-end destination.
steps.instruction: Check the sort confirmation for that parcel to see whether an off-end destination is reported.
steps.expected_result: A sort confirmation is found and indicates whether an off-end destination is reported.
steps.healthy_condition: Sort confirmation information is available and includes off-end destination reporting for the parcel.
steps.failure_condition: No sort confirmation is available, or the confirmation does not provide usable off-end destination information.

actions.label: Review sort confirmation
actions.exact_action: Check the sort confirmation for that parcel to see whether an off-end destination is reported.

stop_or_escalate_if: No sort confirmation is available for the off-end parcel.
The sort confirmation does not show usable off-end destination information.

source_evidence.text: Check the sort confirmation for that parcel to see whether an off-end destination is reported.
Sort confirmations indicate two destinations off the end, allowing some tracking until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
Parcels that go off the end of the sorter can still generate sort confirmations for two off-end destinations, providing limited tracking before records are removed after sort or reject.

steps.title: Distinguish between the two reported off-end destinations
steps.purpose: Use the source-supported sort confirmation information to determine which of the two off-end destinations is being reported.
steps.instruction: Use the source-described sort confirmation information to distinguish between the two off-end destinations.
steps.expected_result: The reported off-end destination is distinguished using the sort confirmation information.
steps.healthy_condition: The sort confirmation clearly indicates one of the two off-end destinations.
steps.failure_condition: The sort confirmation does not allow the user to distinguish the destination, or the exact destination names are not available in the source.

actions.label: Interpret off-end destination
actions.exact_action: Use the source-described sort confirmation information to distinguish between the two off-end destinations.

stop_or_escalate_if: The sort confirmation does not clearly distinguish between the two off-end destinations.
You would need to invent the exact destination names to proceed.

source_evidence.text: Use the source-described sort confirmation information to distinguish between the two off-end destinations.
Sort confirmations indicate two destinations off the end, allowing some tracking until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
Confidence is low because the transcript is truncated and does not provide the exact two destination names or the exact interface used.

steps.title: Record the reported off-end destination
steps.purpose: Capture the reported off-end destination and preserve the source-supported limitation that tracking continues only until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
steps.instruction: Record the reported off-end destination and note that tracking continues through sort confirmations until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
steps.expected_result: The reported off-end destination is documented with the source-supported tracking limitation.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination is recorded and the temporary nature of tracking visibility is noted.
steps.failure_condition: The destination cannot be recorded because confirmation data is missing or ambiguous.

actions.label: Document off-end destination
actions.exact_action: Record the reported off-end destination and note that tracking continues through sort confirmations until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.

stop_or_escalate_if: The parcel record is no longer available because it has been sorted or rejected and removed from tables.

source_evidence.text: Record the reported off-end destination and note that tracking continues through sort confirmations until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
Off the end goes into a Gaylord at Hazleton; sort confirmations indicate two destinations off the end, allowing some tracking until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
Parcels that go off the end of the sorter can still generate sort confirmations for two off-end destinations, providing limited tracking before records are removed after sort or reject.

success_criteria: The user determines the reported off-end destination for the parcel from sort confirmation information.
The user documents that tracking visibility is limited and continues only until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
healthy_conditions: Sort confirmation information is available for the off-end parcel.
The sort confirmation indicates one of two off-end destinations.
The parcel can still be tracked in confirmations before removal from tables.
failure_conditions: No sort confirmation is available for the off-end parcel.
The sort confirmation does not provide enough information to distinguish the off-end destination.
The user would need to invent exact destination names or downstream handling not supported by the source.
The parcel record is no longer available because it has been sorted or rejected and removed from tables.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if no sort confirmation is available for the off-end parcel.
Escalate if the sort confirmation does not clearly distinguish the off-end destination.
Do not invent the exact destination names or downstream handling beyond what the source states.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Off the end goes into a Gaylord at Hazleton; sort confirmations indicate two destinations off the end, allowing some tracking until the parcel is sorted or rejected and removed from tables.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact names of the two off-end destinations.
The source does not identify the exact interface or screen used to view the sort confirmations.
The source does not provide a visual artifact specifically showing the off-end sort confirmation view.
The source evidence is marked low confidence because the transcript is truncated.
